title: Caregiver - PwD Limit Enforcement - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the PwD Limit Enforcement feature on the AT/AD platform. Ensures the system enforces a maximum number of PwDs per caregiver (e.g., max 5). Covers limit enforcement, Add New PwD button disabling when limit reached, informational message display, dynamic slot updates on add/remove, and edge cases including UI-backend mismatch rejection and admin-updated limit reflection.

inScope: Add New PwD button disabled when limit reached
Informational message shown when limit reached
Dynamic slot updates when PwDs are added
Dynamic slot updates when PwDs are removed
UI allows add but backend rejects → error shown
Limit updated by admin → reflected immediately
outOfScope: PwD registration form details (covered in SCRUM-372)
My PwDs dashboard layout (covered in SCRUM-363)

testCases.title: Verify PwD limit is enforced and displayed correctly
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is logged in; PwD limit is configured (e.g., max 5); Caregiver has fewer PwDs than the limit
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver
2. Navigate to My PwDs page
3. Verify the PwD limit is displayed (e.g., 'Max 5 PwDs')
4. Verify current PwD count is displayed (e.g., '2 of 5')
5. Verify remaining slots are shown (e.g., '3 remaining')
6. Add PwDs one by one until the limit is reached
7. After each addition, verify the count updates correctly
8. Verify the limit number itself does not change during additions
expectedResults: PwD limit is clearly displayed on My PwDs page
Current count and remaining slots are accurate
Count updates correctly after each PwD addition
Limit value remains constant (e.g., always shows max 5)
testCases.postconditions: PwD limit display and enforcement is verified

testCases.title: Verify 'Add New PwD' button is disabled when limit is reached
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has added the maximum number of PwDs (e.g., 5 of 5)
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with maximum PwDs
2. Navigate to My PwDs page
3. Verify PwD count shows maximum (e.g., 5/5)
4. Verify remaining slots shows 0
5. Locate the 'Add New PwD' button
6. Verify the button is disabled (greyed out, not clickable)
7. Attempt to click the disabled button
8. Verify no navigation occurs and no action is triggered
9. Verify the 'Add Another PwD' card is also disabled or hidden
expectedResults: Add New PwD button is visually disabled when limit reached
Button does not respond to clicks
No navigation to PwD registration occurs
Add Another PwD card is disabled or hidden
testCases.postconditions: Button disabled state at limit is verified

testCases.title: Verify informational message is shown when limit is reached
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has added the maximum number of PwDs
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with maximum PwDs (5/5)
2. Navigate to My PwDs page
3. Verify an informational message is displayed about the limit
4. Verify the message is clear (e.g., 'You have reached the maximum PwD limit')
5. Verify the message is prominently visible (not hidden or truncated)
6. Verify the message does not block other page functionality
expectedResults: Informational message is displayed when limit is reached
Message clearly states the limit has been reached
Message is visible and readable
Message does not obstruct other page elements
testCases.postconditions: Limit reached informational message is verified

testCases.title: Verify slots update dynamically when a PwD is added
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has fewer PwDs than the limit (e.g., 3 of 5)
steps: 1. Navigate to My PwDs page
2. Note current count (e.g., 3/5, 2 remaining)
3. Add a new PwD via the registration flow
4. Return to My PwDs page
5. Verify PwD count increased by 1 (e.g., 4/5)
6. Verify remaining slots decreased by 1 (e.g., 1 remaining)
7. Verify Add New PwD button is still enabled (if below limit)
8. Add another PwD to reach the limit (5/5)
9. Verify count shows 5/5 and remaining shows 0
10. Verify Add New PwD button is now disabled
11. Verify informational message appears
expectedResults: Count increases by 1 after each PwD addition
Remaining slots decrease by 1 after each addition
Button remains enabled while below limit
Button becomes disabled when limit is reached
Informational message appears at limit
testCases.postconditions: Dynamic slot update on addition is verified

testCases.title: Verify slots update dynamically when a PwD is removed
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has maximum PwDs (5/5); PwD deletion is functional
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with 5/5 PwDs
2. Verify Add New PwD button is disabled
3. Verify informational message is shown
4. Delete one PwD from the care network
5. Verify PwD count decreases to 4/5
6. Verify remaining slots increases to 1
7. Verify Add New PwD button becomes enabled again
8. Verify informational message is removed
9. Verify the Add Another PwD card shows updated remaining count
expectedResults: Count decreases by 1 after PwD removal
Remaining slots increase by 1
Add New PwD button re-enables after dropping below limit
Informational message disappears when below limit
Slot updates happen dynamically without page refresh
testCases.postconditions: Dynamic slot update on removal is verified

testCases.title: Verify UI allows add but backend rejects when limit exceeded shows error
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is at the PwD limit; A race condition or stale UI allows the Add button to appear enabled
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with maximum PwDs (5/5)
2. Open My PwDs page in two browser tabs
3. In Tab 1, verify limit is reached (5/5)
4. In Tab 2, if Add button is somehow enabled (stale state), click it
5. Complete the PwD registration form in Tab 2
6. Submit the registration
7. Verify the backend rejects the submission
8. Verify an error message is shown to the user
9. Verify the error message is clear (e.g., 'PwD limit reached. Cannot add more PwDs.')
10. Verify no PwD is created despite the submission attempt
expectedResults: Backend rejects the add request when limit is exceeded
Clear error message is displayed to the user
No PwD is created beyond the limit
User is not left in a broken state
Error is handled gracefully without crashes
testCases.postconditions: Backend limit enforcement and error handling is verified

testCases.title: Verify admin-updated PwD limit is reflected immediately
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has 5/5 PwDs; Admin has access to change PwD limit
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with 5/5 PwDs (limit = 5)
2. Verify Add New PwD button is disabled
3. Have admin increase the PwD limit to 7
4. Refresh My PwDs page
5. Verify the new limit is reflected (e.g., 5/7)
6. Verify remaining slots shows 2
7. Verify Add New PwD button is now enabled
8. Verify informational message is removed
9. Have admin decrease the limit to 3 (below current count)
10. Refresh My PwDs page
11. Verify the new limit is reflected (e.g., 5/3 or appropriate display)
12. Verify Add New PwD button is disabled
13. Verify informational message is shown
expectedResults: Increased limit is reflected after page refresh
Remaining slots update to reflect new limit
Add button re-enables when limit increases above current count
Decreased limit is reflected after page refresh
Add button disables when limit decreases below or equal to current count
Limit changes are reflected immediately (on refresh)
testCases.postconditions: Admin limit update reflection is verified

testCases.title: Verify limit enforcement with exactly one slot remaining
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver has 4 of 5 PwDs (one slot remaining)
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with 4/5 PwDs
2. Verify remaining slots shows 1
3. Verify Add New PwD button is enabled
4. Verify no limit-reached message is shown
5. Add one more PwD
6. Verify count becomes 5/5
7. Verify remaining slots shows 0
8. Verify Add New PwD button becomes disabled
9. Verify informational message appears
expectedResults: With one slot remaining, button is enabled and no limit message shown
After adding the last PwD, button disables and message appears
Transition from enabled to disabled happens correctly at boundary
testCases.postconditions: Boundary behavior at one slot remaining is verified

testCases.title: Verify limit enforcement with zero PwDs (all slots available)
steps: 1. Log in as caregiver with 0 PwDs
2. Navigate to My PwDs page
3. Verify count shows 0/5
4. Verify remaining slots shows 5
5. Verify Add New PwD button is enabled
6. Verify no limit-reached message is shown
7. Verify empty state message is shown instead
expectedResults: Count shows 0/5 with all slots available
Add New PwD button is enabled
No limit-reached message shown
Empty state message may be shown for zero PwDs

AC2_add_button_disabled.description: When limit reached: Add New PwD disabled

AC3_informational_message.description: When limit reached: Informational message shown

AC4_dynamic_slot_updates.description: Slots update dynamically when PwDs are added/removed

EC1_ui_backend_mismatch.description: UI allows add but backend rejects → error shown

EC2_admin_limit_update.description: Limit updated by admin → reflected immediately

admin_account.purpose: For changing PwD limit in TC_LIMIT_007

assumptions: PwD limit is configured as 5 in QA environment
PwD registration (SCRUM-372) and deletion (SCRUM-381) flows are functional
Admin has ability to change PwD limit for testing
My PwDs page (SCRUM-363) is functional for verifying counts
Multiple caregiver accounts with varying PwD counts are available
dependencies: SCRUM-363: My PwDs page must display limit and slot information
SCRUM-372: PwD registration must be functional for adding PwDs
SCRUM-381: PwD deletion must be functional for removing PwDs
Admin panel must support PwD limit configuration changes

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What is the exact PwD limit? Is it always 5 or configurable per caregiver?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects all test cases - need to know the configured limit

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What happens when admin decreases the limit below the current PwD count?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_LIMIT_007 - are existing PwDs removed or just new additions blocked?

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Does the slot update happen in real-time (WebSocket) or on page refresh?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_LIMIT_004 and TC_LIMIT_005 - dynamic vs refresh-based updates

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What is the exact wording of the informational message when limit is reached?

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_LIMIT_001-004) first - core limit enforcement, button disable, message, dynamic add
High priority tests (005-008) - dynamic remove, UI-backend mismatch, admin update, boundary one slot
Medium priority tests (009) - zero PwDs boundary

estimated_effort.critical_path: 3-4 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: QA environment with AT/AD platform accessible
Multiple caregiver accounts with varying PwD counts (0, 3, 4, 5)
PwD registration and deletion flows functional
Admin access for limit configuration changes
Multiple browser tabs for race condition testing

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria and all edge cases covered
